channel.title: Posts on Bits Thoughts & More, hugo based
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Bits Thoughts & More, hugo based

item.title: Immich in Docker
item.description:
Immich (github) is a media library tool.

At some point I’ve lost my QNAP drives (both at the almost the same time) and wanted to check if I could replace the PhotoStation with an opensource tool.

Immich was one of the candidates but if has a major drawback for me. I dislike that it renames the files you upload/import/sync with it. Although it allows you to download them and the metadata it keeps (in a database) contain all the info and the result you get is the original filenames, you cannot suddenly stop using it and pick the file structure and use it. If you lose the database you have to manually rename and organize all files which can be practically impossible if you have several thousand images or videos in your library.

item.title: Adding Emoji in Hugo Post :smile:
item.description: I’ve seen a particular Hugo theme called hello-friend which I liked and choose to start with it. However, in a post I thought of adding an emoji (a simple :smile:). But it didn’t work, and the reason is that the template did not use a parsing for them. However Hugo supports them, and I only hade to change this in one place in the template to make it work. So in themes/hello-friend/layouts/_default/single.html change :

item.title: adding a post by adding a file
item.description:
So how about just create a new file in the correct file structure and not use the hugo new post? as it turns out it works exactly as expected… So hugo just needs this footer.. and that is all!

+++
title = 'adding a post by adding a file'
date = 2024-04-18T12:56:02+03:00
tags = ['hugo', 'blogging']
lastModDisplay = "Modified:"
draft = "false"
+++

Depending on theme you should be able to see this emoji rendered or not.. :smile:

item.title: Blogging with Hugo
item.description:
Following a guide from Ryan in youtube.. (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LIFvgrRxdt4)

What you need:

  • 2 repos, one for the code of the site and one to deploy (we are going to use github pages)

Those two repos will be joined using git submodule.

In the code repo do: git submodule add -b main <github pages repo> public Where the -b main is the branch of the repo, abd public is the folder that will be linked within the code repo and will point to that branch of the github pages repo.

item.title: letsencrypt and traefik with docker compose
item.description:
problem statement #

In a free tier oci instance I wanted to have a single point of entry with TLS and valid certs I also wanted to have multiple containers served using a wildcard domain. Also needed to be able to add new containers and immediately have them available with cert and fqdn

BOM #

  • traefik
  • duckdns
  • letsencrypt
  • docker compose
  • env-file for docker compose

docker compose #

This is the choice for deploying services.

item.title: elasticsearch notes from a slack app
item.description:
creating a small app with ES as backend #

This is a series about a slack related app where we use ES as the backend for every event we would possibly like to store for analysis.

There are few things we need to consider and the first is related to the fields provided by the slack api that are related to dates and timestamps.

Most of them are ending with ts. By storing all events in ES we can then go and identify them.

item.title: Elastic Components Notes
item.description:
elasticsearch components #

apm server #

running an apm-server for testing purposes in your local machine (here is mac) $GOPATH/bin/apm-server run -v -e -c ~/citrix/testing-apm/apm-server.yml -E apm-server.expvar.enabled=true

config: #

https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/apm/server/current/configuration-monitor.html#_bulk_max_size_6 https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/apm/server/current/elasticsearch-output.html https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/apm/server/current/apm-server-configuration.html https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/apm/server/current/running-on-docker.html

troubleshooting #

https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/apm/server/current/common-problems.html https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/apm/server/current/common-problems.html#queue-full https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/apm/server/current/common-problems.html#request-timed-out https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/apm/server/master/common-problems.html#io-timeout

sizing: #

https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/apm/server/current/sizing-guide.html https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/apm/server/current/processing-performance.html https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/apm/server/current/tune-apm-server.html https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/apm/get-started/7.6/transaction-spans.html

testing/stressing: #

https://github.com/elastic/hey-apm https://github.com/elastic/apm-server/issues/1414

agent: #

https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/apm/agent/python/4.x/configuration.html

test an apm server #

Make 2 files for running the server and the hey-apm benchmarking tool for it

item.title: Partition From Xen Instance
item.description:
How to mount partition from xen instance #

This below describes the way to mount an LVM partition that exists within a partition from a multi-partition disk image.

Let assume our image is called System.img

We will need losetup, kpartx and vgscan,vgchange commands.

With losetup -f you will identify free loopback devices. let say this is loop6.

  1. losetup /dev/loop6 ./System.img

This will mount the disk image in the loop device.

item.title: Docker Registry
item.description:
how to move a docker image to another registry (example push to localhost registry) #

first you need to tag an existing image to another name containing the target registry do a docker images to get a list of images already downloaded command below tags redis:3-alpine as localhost:5000/redis:3-alpine

docker tag redis:3-alpine localhost:5000/redis:3-alpine

then just push the new tag

docker push localhost:5000/redis:3-alpine

and with a curl you can see the output of the catalog in our 127.0.0.1 (localhost) registry # 

curl http://127.0.0.1:5000/v2/_catalog

more info : https://stackoverflow.com/questions/31251356/how-to-get-a-list-of-images-on-docker-registry-v2

item.title: curl auth with cookie
item.description:
use curl to simulate authentication in apis that provide a cookie via auth endpoint (nimbula was using this)

curl -kc ./authtokens -X POST -H "Content-Type: text/plain" -d '{"result": null, "password": "testpassword", "uri": null, "user": "/test/user"}' https://<server>:445/authenticate/

then you can use the cookie from the file to get something:

curl -k -b ./authtokens -X GET "https://<server>:445/node/"
